preview_text:
The model favors Czech Republic with a 53% win probability, placing them as clear favorites on a neutral ground. South Africa's winning chance registers at 18%, creating a gap of roughly 35 percentage points in the favorite's direction.

The draw emerges as a meaningful outcome at 29%, narrowing the margin between outright Czech Republic victory and all other scenarios. The 24-point spread between a Czech Republic win and a South Africa win suggests competitive but asymmetric competition; a draw remains plausible enough to shape match strategy for both sides.

The model and market are closely aligned across all three outcomes. Czech Republic win probabilities differ by fewer than 2 percentage points, draws by under 3 points, and South Africa win chances by roughly 4 points. Polymarket pricing mirrors the sportsbook consensus, indicating broad agreement on the fundamental likelihood structure of this match.
